title: RUSTPY-0019 — contextvars `RefCell<Hamt>` double-borrows under concurrency (`already mutably borrowed`, `contextvars.rs:82`/`:86`)
one_line_repro: see repro.py -- a shared contextvars.Context/ContextVar hammered by threads
notes: The contextvars Hamt (context-variable map) is stored in a RefCell (contextvars.rs), which is !Sync. A Context/ContextVar shared across threads trips overlapping borrow() (:82, borrow_vars) / borrow_mut() (:86, borrow_vars_mut) -- 'RefCell already mutably borrowed' -- and the copy path borrow().clone() (:173) is a third face. Reproduced (worker-thread panic; hit :82 and :86 across runs). CPython's contextvars is thread-safe (runs the same workload clean). SIBLING of RUSTPY-0001 (_thread _local): both are RefCell-used-for-cross-thread-shared-state classes -- RefCell is the wrong primitive for a thread-shareable Python object. Fix = a thread-safe cell (Mutex/RwLock/atomic Hamt). Found by fusil --concurrency-stress (fleet 09), which the single-threaded fleets never hit.
